column13: High flow conditions; water inundating forest on LB and RB. Large (1m) standing waves at o-gauge. Audible cobbles rolling in channel.
column14: Fast velocity; attempted high speed tethered boat mmnt: unsuccessful due to turbulence. Used 150 lb weight on BC1 hanger bar w/ Price AA instead.. Standing waves (large) at mmnt section. Debris hangin up on meter during measurement; required inspection and cleanup every 2nd panel - cleaned meter completely and re-lubed halway through measurement. Difficult measurement. HFC Comments: Control Condition: opeMeasurement Section Codition: OpenWeather Condition: OvecastWind Velocity: calmWind Direction: NO WIN  Corrected M.G.H. includes Sensor Reset Correction of 0.005 and Gage Correction of 0.000 applied to Weighted M.G.H of 2.988 SRC <0.003m on arrival. Logger clock fast 7 min; 07:23 PST WL corresponds to 07:30 HG (uncorrected time). Departure SRC not applied since WL surge was +/- 0.040m during WL observations (stilling well used). Excessive surge - challenging WL observations. Mid-section

Reset both HG (o-line) and HG2 (well submersible) to outside WL at 09:40 PST based on 09:35 SRC (+0.077m). HG2  appears valid. HG2 reset at 09:41 using 09:35 outside WL SRC (+0.016m). column12: Measurement condition : Ice; Cloudy , Freezing Rain , Light Wind Blowing Upstream
column14: Marginal quality measurement due to substantial volume of flow between ice layers near edges. Main channel had large volume of slush with multiple panels having slush to bed. Panel tagmark 15m had bridged ice above a surface layer of high velocity flow and an unmeasurable layer of 0.25m deep effective flow beneath a second deeper layer of ice. The total depth was adjusted (estimated) by 0.12m to account for the second layer of flow.    Corrected M.G.H. includes Sensor Reset Correction of -0.006 and Gage Correction of 0.000 applied to Weighted M.G.H of 1.097 Did not purge due to ice conditions, inaccessible o-line. 1.0m ice at o-line with slight flow beneath ice.

50% ice cover at gauge with anchor ice and heavy flowing slush - "B" conditions. Shore ice on both banks at measurement section resulted in "combined measurement". Several ice condition panels on both banks - wading mmnt through majority of flow. Flowing slush/anchor ice, so could not collect 0.2 depth measurements (ice hitting meter) when depth greater than 0.75m. "0.5" method with 0.88 coefficient for edges; "0.6" method for remainder.   200 metres above at cableway MGH has had sensor reset applied at upload (0.747 + 0.000)Control Condition: SHORE ICE Measurement Section Condition: SHORE ICE Weather Condition:  OVERCAST LIGHT SNOW Sources:Import,Manual
